title: Online Safety Act receives Royal Assent
description: The Online Safety Bill becomes law after years of debate, giving Ofcom the power to regulate user-to-user services and requiring platforms to remove illegal content and protect children【57844258803045†L140-L167】.

title: Government guidance encourages smartphone bans in schools
description: The UK government issues guidance recommending mobile phone bans during school time to reduce distractions and improve learning【553404459537235†L150-L161】.

title: Safer Phones Bill introduced and watered down
description: The Safer Phones Bill is proposed to restrict smartphone use but is watered down after consultation; researchers argue that guided use, not bans, improve learning【968908579016342†L116-L133】.

title: Age verification rules start
description: The Online Safety Act’s age-verification requirements come into force, requiring pornography sites and some platforms to verify users’ ages through methods like credit card checks and facial estimation; Ofcom can fine breaches up to £18m or 10% of turnover【915852258660772†L199-L232】.

title: VPN downloads spike due to age verification
description: Following implementation of age verification, a surge in VPN downloads is reported and some services leave the UK to avoid compliance【728410815567409†L81-L116】.

title: Petition to repeal the Online Safety Act
description: Critics launch a petition arguing that the Act’s privacy and free-speech impacts outweigh its benefits【658625671877330†L160-L195】.

title: Joint ICO/Ofcom age assurance statement
description: UK regulators issue a joint statement insisting that services use highly effective, risk-based age assurance measures beyond self-declaration【668258633734839†L31-L67】.

title: Smartphone bans widespread in schools
description: The Department for Education reports that 99.8% of primary and 90% of secondary schools restrict mobile phone use【71973239089973†L136-L170】.

title: Amendment for under-18 social media restrictions introduced
description: The House of Lords proposes giving the Secretary of State power to restrict under-18s’ access to user-to-user services, raising censorship concerns【32894963678392†L146-L170】.

title: Mental Health Foundation calls for platform safety standards
description: The Mental Health Foundation supports restrictions on social-media platforms unless they meet safety standards, emphasising offline support for young people【472249431651222†L186-L246】.
